Blanco County Stucco Services

Star Stucco of Austin brings professional exterior plaster and stucco craftsmanship to ranch homes, custom builds, and residential properties across Blanco County. Whether you need a full installation on a new custom home or repairs to aging stucco on your Hill Country ranch, our experienced crew delivers results that hold up to the demanding Texas climate.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Stucco Built for Blanco County Living

Blanco County sits right in the heart of the Texas Hill Country, where rolling limestone terrain and wide-open ranch land define the landscape. Homes here face a distinctive set of challenges: intense summer heat that pushes past 100 degrees, sudden downpours that test every exterior surface, and expansive clay soils that shift beneath foundations. Stucco installed in this environment needs to be done right from the start, with proper lath, control joints, and drainage details that account for real-world conditions.

From custom homes along the Blanco River to working ranch properties outside Johnson City, we tailor our approach to each structure. Many homes in this area feature stone-and-stucco combinations that reflect the Hill Country aesthetic, and our team has the skill set to blend new plaster work seamlessly with existing masonry.

We understand that property owners in Blanco County value craftsmanship and durability over quick fixes. That is exactly what we deliver on every project, whether it is a small patch repair or a complete exterior refinish on a sprawling ranch house.

Stucco Services We Offer in Blanco County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Blanco County.

Stucco Repair

Ranch homes and older custom builds across Blanco County develop cracks and wear from constant UV exposure and ground movement. We repair damaged stucco with proper bonding techniques and color-matched finish coats that restore the original look without leaving visible patch lines.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

New construction in the Johnson City and Round Mountain area deserves stucco that performs for decades. We install traditional three-coat and one-coat stucco systems with attention to moisture barriers, weep screeds, and expansion control that prevent the problems we see on poorly installed homes throughout the Hill Country.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

The expansive soils common in Blanco County cause foundation shifts that telegraph into stucco surfaces as hairline and structural cracks. Our repair process addresses both the cosmetic damage and the underlying cause, using flexible sealants and reinforced mesh where movement is likely to continue.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Heavy rains that sweep through the Hill Country can drive moisture behind improperly sealed stucco, causing hidden rot and mold. We trace the source of water intrusion, replace compromised sheathing and lath, and rebuild the stucco envelope with proper flashing and kickout details.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

Many Blanco County homeowners choose hand-troweled plaster finishes that complement the natural stone and timber elements popular in Hill Country architecture. We offer smooth, skip-trowel, and Santa Barbara finishes applied by skilled plasterers who take pride in creating surfaces that look as good up close as they do from a distance.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

Exterior Insulation and Finish Systems provide added energy efficiency for Blanco County homes that face brutal summer heat. We install and repair EIFS with proper drainage planes and sealant joints, giving your home a lightweight finish with genuine insulating value.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

Wineries, event venues, and retail spaces along the Highway 290 corridor rely on attractive stucco exteriors to draw visitors. We handle commercial projects of all sizes, from storefront facelifts in downtown Blanco to large-scale new construction for hospitality properties.

Learn More

Why Blanco County Property Owners Trust Star Stucco

Deep familiarity with Blanco County soil conditions and how they affect stucco performance over time

Experienced with ranch-style and custom home construction common throughout the Hill Country

Color matching and texture blending that makes repairs virtually invisible on existing walls

Honest project scoping so you only pay for the work your property actually needs

Licensed and insured crew that arrives on time and keeps your property clean throughout the job

Written warranties on materials and workmanship for lasting peace of mind

Local Expertise

We understand Blanco County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every Blanco County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Blanco County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Blanco County

Blanco County homes face specific stucco challenges tied to the local climate and terrain. Here are the most common issues we address for homeowners in this area.

Cracks

Limestone bedrock and reactive clay pockets throughout Blanco County cause uneven ground movement that opens cracks in rigid stucco surfaces. We see this frequently on ranch homes built on sloped lots near creek beds where moisture levels in the soil fluctuate dramatically between seasons.

Bubbling or soft stucco

Trapped moisture beneath the stucco coat creates soft spots and bubbling, especially on north-facing walls of older homes near Blanco and Johnson City where morning dew lingers and shade slows evaporation throughout the cooler months.

Water stains

Mineral-rich well water from Blanco County aquifers leaves chalky white and rust-colored streaks on stucco surfaces when it runs off rooflines or leaks from improperly routed gutters and downspouts.

Window and door leaks

Wind-driven rain during spring storms finds its way behind stucco at window and door openings where caulk has dried out or flashing was never properly integrated into the weather-resistant barrier.

Failing patches

Previous repair attempts using mismatched materials or improper surface preparation often fail within a year or two, leaving raised edges and color differences that are more noticeable than the original damage.

Separation around trim

Wood and composite trim on Hill Country homes expands and contracts at different rates than the surrounding stucco, opening gaps that allow insects and moisture to enter the wall assembly.

Impact damage

Hailstorms, falling tree branches from live oaks, and even stray rocks kicked up by mowers leave dents and chips in stucco that expose the underlying lath to corrosion if left unrepaired.

Faded or stained exterior walls

Years of unrelenting Texas sun bleach the pigment out of colored stucco finishes, while biological growth in shaded areas under eaves and porches adds dark streaks that make the home look neglected.

How We Work in Blanco County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule inspection

Contact us by phone or through our website to set up a convenient time for an on-site visit. We will work around your schedule and can often get out to Blanco County properties within a few business days.

2

Review scope

Our technician examines every wall surface, probing for hidden moisture, checking control joints, and documenting all areas that need attention. We take photos and measurements so nothing gets missed.

3

Get estimate

You receive a detailed written estimate that breaks down labor, materials, and timeline. We explain exactly what we recommend and why, so you can make a confident decision without feeling pressured.

4

Complete work

Once approved, our crew arrives with all materials and equipment, completes the work efficiently, and walks you through the finished result. We clean up thoroughly and leave your property looking better than we found it.
